title: Changes in the determinants of supply
description: Practice what you have learned about changes in the supply curve when a determinant of supply changes in this exercise.
topic_path: Changes in the determinants of supply

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements accurately describes how an event will impact supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in costs of production will cause an increase in the supply curve.
An increase in demand will cause an increase in supply.
An increase in price will cause supply to increase.
A decrease in price will cause supply to increase.
A decrease in resource costs will cause an increase in the supply curve.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following could cause the movement indicated by the arrow in the graph shown here?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The figure presents a graph of two supply curves in the first quadrant of the cartesian plane. The horizontal axis is labeled Q and the vertical axis is labeled P. One curve is labeled S 1 and starts at the vertical axis above the origin at coordinates 0 and 20 and moves up and to the right and ends at coordinates 80 and 60. The second curve is labeled S 2 and is directly above and parallel to the first curve and starts at coordinates 0 and 40 and moves up and to the right and ends at coordinates 80 and 80. There is an arrow pointing to the left from the first curve to the second curve.
options.caption: Two supply curves

choices.content: Increased government regulations of producers
Seller's expectation that the good's price will fall in the future
Reduced government regulation of producers
A decrease in consumer income
Lower worker wages

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following could cause the shift from S1 to S2 shown in the graph shown?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The figure presents a graph of two supply curves in the first quadrant of the cartesian plane. The horizontal axis is labeled Q and the vertical axis is labeled P. One curve is labeled S 1 and starts at the vertical axis above the origin and moves up and to the right. The second curve is labeled S 2 and is directly below and parallel to the first curve and moves up and to the right. There is an arrow pointing to the right from the first curve to the second curve.
options.caption: Two supply curves

choices.content: An increases in taxes on producers
A reduction in raw material prices
A decrease in the number of producers in the market
Higher worker wages
An increase in consumer incomes

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that basketballs and volleyballs use identical resources to produce and can be made with the same workers and machinery.

**How would an increase in the price of basketballs affect the production of basketballs and volleyballs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Supply of volleyballs will decrease
Quantity supplied of basketballs will not be affected.
Supply of volleyballs will increase
Quantity supplied of volleyballs will increase
Quantity supplied of basketballs will decrease

assessment_items.question_content:
Land in the United States can be used to produce either soybeans or corn. The price of soybean fertilizer has fallen due to cheaper imports from Ghana.

**What will change for soybeans and corn?**

[[☃ radio 3]]

choices.content: Supply of both corn and soybeans will increase
Supply of soybeans will decrease and supply of corn will increase
Supply of soybeans will increase and the supply of corn will decrease
Quantity supplied of corn and quantity supplied of soybeans will both increase
Quantity supplied of corn and quantity supplied of soybeans will both decrease

assessment_items.question_content:
Anthony’s Ephemera Emporium sells signed photos of cryptids like Bigfoot, chupacabra, and other mythical creatures. These photos are substitutes in production.

**If Anthony expects the price of signed photos of Bigfoot to increase, what happens?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of signed photos of chupacabras decrease.
The supply of signed photos of Bigfoot increases
The supply of signed photos of chupacabras increases
The supply of signed photos of Bigfoot decreases
The demand for signed photos of either good increase.

assessment_items.question_content:
Coffee bean merchants notice that coffee prices are at a historic low today, but they expect the price of coffee beans to increase in the next six months.

**How will the expectation of an increase in future prices most likely affect the supply of coffee beans on the market today?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Supply will decrease.
Supply will increase.
There will be a movement along the same supply curve to a new higher quantity supplied.
There will be a movement along the same supply curve to a new lower quantity supplied.
There is no change in supply.

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that crayons can be stored indefinitely.

**What happens today if producers of crayons expect to get higher prices in the future?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of crayons decreases
The quantity supplied of crayons decreases
The supply and demand for crayons both increase
The supply of crayons increases
The demand for crayons increases

assessment_items.question_content:
The sellers of kitten mittens expect the price of kitten mittens to decrease during the summer months. Assume kitten mittens can be stored indefinitely.

**What happens to the supply of kitten mittens the preceding winter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of kitten mittens increases.

The quantity demanded of kitten mittens increases
.
The quantity supplied of kitten mittens decreases.
The supply and demand of kitten mittens both increase.
The demand for kitten mittens increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
Pam sells hand-shaped soaps at farmer's market. She recently created a revolutionary new soap production technique that doubled her production without having to use more resources.

**What changes as a result of Pam’s innovation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of hand-shaped soap increases.
The quantity supplied of hand-shaped soap decreases.
The supply of hand-shaped soap decreases.
The demand for soap increases.
The demand for soap decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following could cause the shift from S1 to S2 shown here?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The figure presents a graph of two supply curves in the first quadrant of the cartesian plane. The horizontal axis is labeled Q and the vertical axis is labeled P. One curve is labeled S 1 and starts at the vertical axis above the origin and to the right. The second curve is labeled S 2 and is directly below and parallel to the first curve and moves up and to the right. There is an arrow pointing to the right from the first curve to the second curve.
options.caption: Two supply curves

choices.content: An increases in taxes on producers
An increase in raw material prices
A decrease in the number of producers in the market
Higher worker wages
An improvement in technology used in production

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following could cause the shift shown in this graph?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The figure presents a graph of two supply curves in the first quadrant of the cartesian plane. The horizontal axis is labeled Q and the vertical axis is labeled P. One curve is labeled S 1 and starts at the vertical axis above the origin and to the right. The second curve is labeled S 2 and is directly above and parallel to the first curve and moves up and to the right. There is an arrow pointing to the left from the first curve to the second curve.
options.caption: Two supply curves

choices.content: a reduction in regulations
an increase in the cost of an input to production
an increase in the number of producers in the market
lower cost of labor
sellers expect a lower price in the future
